title: Animations & Transitions
subtitle: make slides move magically!
overview: This session introduces students to PowerPoint animations and transitions. Students will learn how to make text, images, and slides move creatively to create engaging presentations.

images._note: Add photos from animation practice sessions, student presentations, and PowerPoint activities.

gallery.caption: Students adding animations to text and images

keyTopics: Types of Animation Effects — Entrance, Emphasis, Exit, Motion Paths
Popular Transitions — Fade, Push, Wipe, Morph
Adding sound and effects to slides

activity.title: Create an Animated Presentation

quiz.q: What is an Animation in PowerPoint?
options: Making text or objects move on a slide
quiz.explanation: Animations make objects like text and images move or appear in different ways on a slide.

quiz.q: What is a Transition in PowerPoint?
options: Movement between one slide and another
quiz.explanation: Transitions are visual effects that happen when moving from one slide to the next.

quiz.q: Which tab is used to add animations?
quiz.explanation: The Animations tab contains all animation effects and settings.

quiz.q: Which of these is a transition effect?
quiz.explanation: Fade is a popular slide transition effect in PowerPoint.

quiz.q: Which animation makes an object appear on the slide?
quiz.explanation: Entrance animations make objects appear on the slide.

quiz.q: Which animation makes an object disappear?
quiz.explanation: Exit animations remove objects from the slide.

quiz.q: What does the Animation Pane do?
quiz.explanation: The Animation Pane helps manage the order and timing of animations.

quiz.q: Which transition creates a smooth blending effect?
quiz.explanation: Morph creates smooth movement and transformation between slides.

quiz.q: Which option controls how long an animation lasts?
quiz.explanation: Timing settings control animation speed and duration.

quiz.q: What is a Motion Path animation?
options: Moves an object along a path
quiz.explanation: Motion Path animations allow objects to move in a custom direction or path.

quiz.q: Which feature makes presentations more attractive?
quiz.explanation: Animations and transitions make presentations engaging and interactive.

quiz.q: Which shortcut starts a slideshow from beginning?
quiz.explanation: F5 starts the slideshow from the beginning.

quiz.q: Which transition effect slides one slide over another?
quiz.explanation: Push transition moves one slide over another smoothly.
Animations are applied to text, images, shapes, and other objects.

quiz.q: Why should animations be used carefully?
options: Too many effects can distract viewers
quiz.explanation: Using too many animations can make presentations distracting and confusing.
